states_list.name: INIT
states_list.description: Initial connection established, awaiting authentication credentials.

states_list.name: AUTH_PENDING
states_list.description: User identity provided, awaiting password (PASS command).

states_list.name: AUTHENTICATED
states_list.description: User successfully authenticated, all non-privileged commands allowed.

states_list.name: DATA_TRANSFER
states_list.description: Actively transferring file or directory listing data.

states_list.name: RENAME_PENDING
states_list.description: A rename operation is partially complete, waiting for the destination name.

invariants_list.rule: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command in the same session.
A successful USER/PASS sequence must occur before LIST, RETR, or STOR commands.
A PASV or PORT command should precede the initiation of data transfer commands like LIST, RETR, or STOR.
If a PASS command fails (e.g., 530), the client may send another USER command to retry authentication.
An RNTO command must be preceded by an RNFR command.
The QUIT command is valid at any point after the initial connection response.
PASS.PASS: The PASS command must follow the USER command.
PORT.address_info: The PORT command argument must be six comma-separated integers (h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2).
TYPE.type_code: The TYPE command parameter must be 'A' (ASCII) or 'I' (Image/Binary).
RNTO.filename: RNTO must immediately follow a successful RNFR command.

invariants_created: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command in the same session.
A successful USER/PASS sequence must occur before LIST, RETR, or STOR commands.
A PASV or PORT command should precede the initiation of data transfer commands like LIST, RETR, or STOR.
If a PASS command fails (e.g., 530), the client may send another USER command to retry authentication.
An RNTO command must be preceded by an RNFR command.
The QUIT command is valid at any point after the initial connection response.
PASS.PASS: The PASS command must follow the USER command.
PORT.address_info: The PORT command argument must be six comma-separated integers (h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2).
TYPE.type_code: The TYPE command parameter must be 'A' (ASCII) or 'I' (Image/Binary).
RNTO.filename: RNTO must immediately follow a successful RNFR command.

status_changes.rationale: The USER command with 331 response directly confirms the transition to a state awaiting authentication.
Response code 230 confirms the completion of authentication and transition to a logged-in state.
Response code 530 indicates a failure that maintains the session in an unauthenticated but initialized state.
Response code 150 indicates the start of a data transfer for directory listing.
Response code 150 indicates the start of a data transfer for file retrieval.
Response code 150 indicates the start of a data transfer for file storage.
Response code 350 explicitly transitions the session into a state waiting for the rename destination.
Response code 250 indicates the completion of the rename and return to the primary authenticated state.
Response code 221 confirms the closure of the session.
The REIN command resets the session to a fresh state (220), effectively returning to INIT.
Response code 221 confirms that sessions can be closed before authentication.

status_changes.description: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command in the same session.
status_changes.rationale: Standard FTP requirement documented in the spec agent output and reinforced by the 331 response code requirement for password.

status_changes.description: A successful USER/PASS sequence must occur before LIST, RETR, or STOR commands.
status_changes.rationale: Standard FTP state-machine behavior where data commands require an authenticated state.

status_changes.description: A PASV or PORT command should precede the initiation of data transfer commands like LIST, RETR, or STOR.
status_changes.rationale: Protocol requirement to establish the data channel parameters before data command execution.

status_changes.description: If a PASS command fails (e.g., 530), the client may send another USER command to retry authentication.
status_changes.rationale: Standard error handling and retry logic in FTP sessions.

status_changes.description: An RNTO command must be preceded by an RNFR command.
status_changes.rationale: Standard two-step file renaming sequence in FTP as evidenced by the 350 response code.

status_changes.description: The QUIT command is valid at any point after the initial connection response.
status_changes.rationale: The QUIT command is designed to terminate the session from any state.

status_changes.description: PASS.PASS: The PASS command must follow the USER command.
status_changes.rationale: Duplicate of the previous invariant but correctly identifies the field-level requirement.

status_changes.description: PORT.address_info: The PORT command argument must be six comma-separated integers (h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2).
status_changes.rationale: Standard syntax for the FTP PORT command parameters.

status_changes.description: TYPE.type_code: The TYPE command parameter must be 'A' (ASCII) or 'I' (Image/Binary).
status_changes.rationale: Most common types in FTP; while others exist, these are the primary functional ones.

status_changes.description: RNTO.filename: RNTO must immediately follow a successful RNFR command.
status_changes.rationale: Strict sequencing requirement for renaming operations in FTP.
